title: centaurea jacea var. genuina
intro: Centaurea jacea var. genuina is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is commonly known as yellow knapweed or lesser knapweed. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and promoting the healing of ulcers. Centaurea jacea var. genuina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
